topics: Names of birds, animals and flowers in Sanskrit
God as the great artist (महान् चित्रकारः)
subject: Sanskrit

sections.heading: 🌿 The Quest Begins — A Garden Full of Colours!
sections.body: Imagine visiting a beautiful garden like the Amrit Udyan near Rashtrapati Bhavan in Delhi. Your quest today is to notice colours all around you! In this chapter, students and their teacher go on exactly this adventure — they look at flowers, birds, animals and discover that nature is bursting with colours. Just like when you walk into a mela and see red, yellow, green and blue everywhere — nature's mela is even more colourful!
keyPoints: पुष्पाणि = flowers, पर्णानि = leaves, जन्तवः = animals, खगाः = birds
The garden has thousands of seasonal flowers of many colours — this is called वर्णमय (colourful)

sections.heading: 🎨 Casting the Colour Spell — Who Sees What?
sections.body: In the classroom quest, each student notices a different colour. Shraddha loves green — she sees green leaves and even a green parrot! Medha spots a crow and a cuckoo — both are black (कृष्णः). Manisha sees the red hibiscus flower (जपापुष्पम्) and points out that the parrot's beak is also red. Just like in Holi, where everyone notices their favourite colour first, each student in the story notices what they love most!
keyPoints: हरितः = green, कृष्णः = black, रक्तः = red, नीलः = blue, पीतः = yellow, श्वेतः = white
जपापुष्पम् (hibiscus) is red; काकः (crow) and पिकः (cuckoo) are both black

sections.heading: 🦢 Power of Observation — Spots More Colours!
sections.body: The teacher asks students to look even more carefully — this is true wizard-level observation! Manjul says roses (पाटलपुष्पम्) come in yellow, white, purple and pink colours. The teacher points to a swan (हंसः) — it is white (श्वेतः). A heron (बकः) is also white. Even the teacher's coat is white! Just like when you look at the Indian flag — saffron on top, white in the middle and green at the bottom — nature also has layers of colours everywhere.
keyPoints: हंसः = swan (white), बकः = heron (white), शशकः = rabbit (white)
पाटलपुष्पम् = rose, which comes in पीत (yellow), श्वेत (white), नीललोहित (purple), केसर (saffron) and पाटल (pink) colours

sections.heading: 🌈 The Rainbow Realm — Nature's Greatest Masterpiece
sections.body: Manjul then points to the rainbow — इन्द्रधनुः — which has seven colours! The teacher agrees and says all of nature is colourful and that is what makes our world beautiful. Think about watching a sunset from your terrace — the sky turns orange, pink and purple all at once. That is the magic of colours! The teacher then asks a big question — who is the great artist who painted all this?
keyPoints: इन्द्रधनुः = rainbow, which has सप्त वर्णाः = seven colours
Colours make our जीवनम् (life) मनोरम (beautiful and pleasant)

sections.heading: 🐉 The Greatest Wizard Revealed — सः एव महान् चित्रकारः!
sections.body: The whole class shouts together — परमेश्वरः! परमेश्वरः! God is the greatest artist who coloured every flower, every bird, every leaf and every sky. Just like no human painter can paint an entire forest with millions of different shades, only God can do that. The chapter teaches us to look at nature with wonder and feel grateful — because every colour around us is a gift from the greatest artist in the universe!
keyPoints: परमेश्वरः = God, who is called महान् चित्रकारः — the greatest artist
Every natural thing — flowers, birds, rainbow, sky — gets its colour from God's masterful creation

summary: In this heroic quest, Class 6 students explore a garden and discover the magical realm of colours in Sanskrit. Through a fun classroom conversation, they learn the Sanskrit names for colours like हरितः (green), रक्तः (red), पीतः (yellow), श्वेतः (white) and कृष्णः (black), along with names of flowers like जपापुष्पम् and पाटलपुष्पम्, birds like काकः, पिकः, हंसः and बकः, and the rainbow इन्द्रधनुः. The chapter builds the power of observation and ends with the wonderful realisation that God — परमेश्वरः — is the greatest artist of all, having coloured our entire world with infinite beauty. Every young wizard who learns this chapter gains the mastery to see and name the colourful world around them in Sanskrit!
